task_objective: Add diagnostic logging so users can determine from the logs why the plugin made auto-sync decisions and why the status bar showed, hid, or never appeared
tests_added: src/surfaces/autoSyncStatusSurface.suppression.test.ts (4 tests: running-status timeout log, suppressed-final-result log, auto-hide schedule debug log, unhandled-result-status log)
src/controllers/gameLifecycleController.logging.test.ts (2 tests: pre-check status bar suppression reasons, stale-epoch drop logging)
design_decisions: Audit-first approach: identified silent decision points rather than blanket-adding logs. The biggest user-facing mysteries were completeAutoSyncStatus silently dropping the final result after the 10s running-status timeout, the epoch guard silently dropping stale publishes, and the controller never explaining why the pre-RPC 'VERIFYING GAME SAVE' bar was suppressed.

compare_recency now logs its verdict (and the restore-preview change value) at info level via the module LOGGER, which DeckyLogHandler routes into the diagnostic ring buffer and decky logger, so the verdict appears in the in-plugin Log modal.
check_game_exit extracts and logs the full preview summary (decision/change/files/registry counts) before any skip decision, so every exit skip reason can be corroborated.
Conflict-prompt and backup_differs direction logs now include the local/backup timestamps that drove the decision.
Log levels: info for behavior-visible decisions (verdicts, suppression, timeout-hide); debug for mechanical detail (auto-hide scheduling, stale-epoch drops, unhandled result fallthrough).
Kept gameLifecycleController.tsx within its 550-line module size budget by compacting the new helpers rather than raising the budget.
results: All quality gates pass: ruff check/format clean, ty clean, 503 backend tests pass, 85 frontend vitest tests pass, tsc --noEmit clean.
